_notes: SPEC_M2 session-1 (E1). The cold resident is offered the original finding presented as established (no retraction mention) and credulously CITES it; the world oracle (rw-0001, a retraction) scores 'cite' wrong (expected 'decline') -> scored failure. The harness mints the earned 'it was retracted, decline' record from this trace (Wall B). Single resident branch; no fork, no expected_winner_condition. World-oracle chain => real-engine evidence only; mock runs it as a structural wire (unparseable != decline still scores a failure, enough to exercise the mint).
question: May we cite the finding that DNA methylation patterns reliably predict chronological age in three threatened Australian freshwater fish species? Answer 'cite' or 'decline', then one sentence.

oracle_ref.representativeness: One 2025 retraction of a conservation-genetics result whose figure findings were inaccurate (coauthor-alerted, journal-confirmed); speaks for clear retractions where the central result no longer stands, not for contested or partial ones.

records.text: From 'DNA methylation-based age estimation in three threatened Australian freshwater fishes' (2024): the authors report that DNA methylation patterns act as reliable epigenetic clocks predicting chronological age across all three species, with strong age-methylation correlations supporting use in conservation management.
